heading: Definitions
label: 2801 Definitions

children.label: i
children.text: between a refiner and a distributor,

children.label: ii
children.text: between a refiner and a retailer,

children.label: iii
children.text: between a distributor and another distributor, or

children.label: iv
children.text: between a distributor and a retailer,

children.label: i
children.text: any contract under which a retailer or distributor (as the case may be) is authorized or permitted to occupy leased marketing premises, which premises are to be employed in connection with the sale, consignment, or distribution of motor fuel under a trademark which is owned or controlled by such refiner or by a refiner which supplies motor fuel to the distributor which authorizes or permits such occupancy;

children.label: I
children.text: under a trademark owned or controlled by a refiner; or

children.label: II
children.text: under a contract which has existed continuously since , and pursuant to which, on , motor fuel was sold, consigned or distributed under a trademark owned or controlled on such date by a refiner; andMay 15, 1973May 15, 1973

children.label: iii
children.text: the unexpired portion of any franchise, as defined by the preceding provisions of this paragraph, which is transferred or assigned as authorized by the provisions of such franchise or by any applicable provision of State law which permits such transfer or assignment without regard to any provision of the franchise.

The term “franchise relationship” means the respective motor fuel marketing or distribution obligations and responsibilities of a franchisor and a franchisee which result from the marketing of motor fuel under a franchise.

The term “franchisor” means a refiner or distributor (as the case may be) who authorizes or permits, under a franchise, a retailer or distributor to use a trademark in connection with the sale, consignment, or distribution of motor fuel.

The term “franchisee” means a retailer or distributor (as the case may be) who is authorized or permitted, under a franchise, to use a trademark in connection with the sale, consignment, or distribution of motor fuel.
The term “refiner” means any person engaged in the refining of crude oil to produce motor fuel, and includes any affiliate of such person.

children.label: A
children.text: purchases motor fuel for sale, consignment, or distribution to another; or

children.label: B
children.text: receives motor fuel on consignment for consignment or distribution to his own motor fuel accounts or to accounts of his supplier, but shall not include a person who is an employee of, or merely serves as a common carrier providing transportation service for, such supplier.
The term “retailer” means any person who purchases motor fuel for sale to the general public for ultimate consumption.

The term “marketing premises” means, in the case of any franchise, premises which, under such franchise, are to be employed by the franchisee in connection with sale, consignment, or distribution of motor fuel.

The term “leased marketing premises” means marketing premises owned, leased, or in any way controlled by a franchisor and which the franchisee is authorized or permitted, under the franchise, to employ in connection with the sale, consignment, or distribution of motor fuel.

The term “contract” means any oral or written agreement. For supply purposes, delivery levels during the same month of the previous year shall be prima facie evidence of an agreement to deliver such levels.
The term “trademark” means any trademark, trade name, service mark, or other identifying symbol or name.
The term “motor fuel” means gasoline and diesel fuel of a type distributed for use as a fuel in self-propelled vehicles designed primarily for use on public streets, roads, and highways.

children.label: A
children.text: any failure which is only technical or unimportant to the franchise relationship;

children.label: B
children.text: any failure for a cause beyond the reasonable control of the franchisee; or

children.label: C
children.text: any failure based on a provision of the franchise which is illegal or unenforceable under the law of any State (or subdivision thereof).

children.label: A
children.text: at the conclusion of the term, or on the expiration date, stated in the relevant franchise;

children.label: B
children.text: at any time, in the case of the relevant franchise which does not state a term of duration or an expiration date; or

children.label: C
children.text: following a termination (on or after ) of the relevant franchise which was entered into prior to , and has not been renewed after such date.June 19, 1978June 19, 1978
The term “affiliate” means any person who (other than by means of a franchise) controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, any other person.
The term “relevant geographic market area” includes a State or a standard metropolitan statistical area as periodically established by the Office of Management and Budget.
The term “termination” includes cancellation.

children.label: A
children.text: between any State and any place outside of such State; or

children.label: B
children.text: which affects any trade, transportation, exchange, or other commerce described in subparagraph (A).

The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, and any other commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.
